questions.stem: The structure shown in the lower-right quadrant of this comparison differs from the other three antibiotic classes depicted. What structural property distinguishes it?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Its beta-lactam ring is monocyclic, not fused to a second ring

choices.label: B
choices.text: It has a five-membered thiazolidine ring fused to the beta-lactam

choices.label: C
choices.text: It lacks a beta-lactam ring entirely and acts as a glycopeptide

choices.label: D
choices.text: It contains a six-membered dihydrothiazine ring fused to the beta-lactam

choices.label: E
choices.text: It replaces the beta-lactam ring sulfur with a carbon atom

explanation.summary: Monobactams (lower right) are the only class shown with a solitary, monocyclic beta-lactam ring not fused to a second ring. Penicillins have a fused thiazolidine ring, cephalosporins a fused dihydrothiazine ring, and carbapenems a fused carbon-containing ring. This monocyclic structure underlies aztreonam's minimal cross-reactivity with penicillins.
explanation.whyCorrect: Monobactams (lower right) are the only class shown with a solitary, monocyclic beta-lactam ring not fused to a second ring. Penicillins have a fused thiazolidine ring, cephalosporins a fused dihydrothiazine ring, and carbapenems a fused carbon-containing ring. This monocyclic structure underlies aztreonam's minimal cross-reactivity with penicillins.

questions.stem: Of the three adverse reactions illustrated here for carbapenems and monobactams, the central one is most likely to occur with which specific agent, particularly in renal impairment?

explanation.summary: The central panel depicts seizures. Carbapenems can lower the seizure threshold, but the risk is greatest with imipenem/cilastatin, especially at high doses or when drug accumulates due to renal impairment. Meropenem is preferred clinically because of its lower seizure risk.
explanation.whyCorrect: The central panel depicts seizures. Carbapenems can lower the seizure threshold, but the risk is greatest with imipenem/cilastatin, especially at high doses or when drug accumulates due to renal impairment. Meropenem is preferred clinically because of its lower seizure risk.

questions.stem: This schematic of the bacterial cell envelope highlights an enzyme acting between the NAG-NAM glycan strands. What is the direct consequence when beta-lactams bind that enzyme?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Peptidoglycan cross-links fail to form, weakening the wall and causing osmotic lysis

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ribosomal protein synthesis is halted at the 50S subunit

choices.label: C
choices.text: The plasma membrane becomes permeable to protons

choices.label: D
choices.text: The 30S ribosomal subunit is blocked, causing misreading

choices.label: E
choices.text: DNA gyrase is inhibited, blocking chromosome replication

explanation.summary: The enzyme shown (a penicillin-binding protein) is a transpeptidase that cross-links adjacent NAM-NAG peptidoglycan strands. Beta-lactams irreversibly inhibit it, so without cross-linking the wall cannot withstand osmotic pressure and the growing bacterium lyses.
explanation.whyCorrect: The enzyme shown (a penicillin-binding protein) is a transpeptidase that cross-links adjacent NAM-NAG peptidoglycan strands. Beta-lactams irreversibly inhibit it, so without cross-linking the wall cannot withstand osmotic pressure and the growing bacterium lyses.
